Section 01

What We Heard

Key takeaways from our conversation with Dr. Salinas — the foundation for everything in this proposal.

The Practice

Core specialty is Degenerative Disc Disease (DDD) — highest volume of surgeries and biggest growth opportunity in DFW
400+ Google reviews, 4.7 rating — strong social proof not yet leveraged across digital channels
17 years serving DFW — deep local roots and reputation built on outcomes
Revision/failed back syndrome expertise — differentiated capability no Frisco competitor is marketing

The Opportunity

Three patient pipelines: in-person PCP outreach, patient direct via online/insurance, and digital marketing — each needs its own strategy
Conservative-first philosophy is a powerful differentiator patients and referring physicians actively seek
Website needs SEO optimization and condition-specific content pages to capture search traffic from patients researching symptoms and treatments
Social presence and paid media are not mature — expanding both is essential to drive new patient acquisition beyond referral-only growth
The core insight: The quality of care at Frisco Spine is not the gap. It's the visibility of that quality. Dr. Salinas's credentials, outcomes, and patient satisfaction are at or above every competitor — but the digital presence, social media, and paid media channels don't yet reflect it.
Section 02

Who We're Reaching

Four patient personas drive the strategy. Each searches differently, trusts differently, and converts through different channels.

MA
Maria A. — Active Professional Primary
  • Age 38–50, employed, worsening back pain
  • Starts with symptom searches → provider comparison
  • Insurance terms in searches signal high intent
  • Compares on Google + Healthgrades before calling
BT
Barbara T. — Caregiver Deep Researcher
  • Age 55–70, researching for parent or spouse
  • Validates credentials on Healthgrades and Vitals
  • YouTube is her second research channel
  • Long session times on comprehensive content
JM
James M. — Second Opinion High Value
  • Age 45–60, uncertain about surgical recommendation
  • Searches for second opinions and conservative alternatives
  • Lowest volume but highest per-lead revenue
  • Short decision window, high anxiety
YD
Yolanda D. — Digital Native Emerging
  • Age 28–40, discovers via TikTok and Instagram
  • Conversational, question-based searches
  • Will not call — expects online forms
  • Mobile-first, searches at night
77%
search online before booking
21×
more likely to convert within 5 min
84%
check profiles before scheduling
61%
decline referral on weak presence

Section 04

Organic Search & SEO

SEO takes 4–9 months to build momentum, but delivers $22 return per $1 invested and compounds monthly.

Website Optimization

Semantic HTML structure — proper heading hierarchy, schema markup for MedicalBusiness, Physician, LocalBusiness
Page speed and Core Web Vitals — fast pages rank higher and convert better
Mobile-first design — 60%+ of spine patients search on mobile
Internal linking — condition pages cross-link to bio, form, and related conditions

Beyond the Website

Google Business Profile — optimize listing, services, Q&A, posts, photos for local map pack
Indexing & crawl management — sitemaps, error fixes, condition page discoverability
Review generation — automated post-visit requests to build count and rating
Directory consistency — NAP consistent across Healthgrades, Vitals, Yelp, medical directories
53%
of traffic from organic search
$22
return per $1 on SEO over time
14.6%
SEO close rate vs 1.7% outbound
Section 05

Paid Media — Google & Social Ads

Paid ads deliver leads from day one. Suggested spend: $1,000–$2,000/month (billed separately, no markup).

Google Search Ads — high-intent patients
Sponsored · Google Search
DDD Specialist in Frisco, TX — Conservative Treatment First
friscospine.com/degenerative-disc-disease
Board-certified neurosurgeon. Fellowship trained at Cedars-Sinai. 400+ five-star reviews. We exhaust every non-surgical option before recommending surgery.
Request Consultation
Sponsored · Google Search
Had Back Surgery That Didn't Work? — Revision Spine Specialist
friscospine.com/second-opinion
Dr. Salinas specializes in revision spine surgery for patients with failed back syndrome. 17 years experience. Get a second opinion.
Get a Second Opinion
Facebook / Instagram — DDD campaign
FS
Frisco Spine
Sponsored
Back pain that radiates into your leg isn't just "bad posture." It may be a treatable disc condition. Dr. Salinas reviews every case personally — bring your existing MRI.

#FriscoSpine #BackPainRelief
ROI path: Click → DDD page → consultation form. Est. CPL: $18–$45. Single converted lead returns 400–1,000× ad spend.
Targeting
Ages 38–55 · DFW 100-mile radius · DDD, spine surgery, back pain, second opinion keywords
Budget Split
Google: $800–$1,200/mo · Social: $200–$800/mo · Billed at cost, no markup
Retargeting
Every click builds warm audience. Non-converters see second-opinion retargeting within 7 days
Powered by
SuperSymm Smart Media
Connected channels
Google Search, Facebook, Instagram, and retargeting don't run in silos. Every channel shares audience data, conversion signals, and creative learnings — so performance compounds across the entire media mix.
Contextual targeting
Ads are matched to the patient's stage in the care journey — awareness, research, or decision. Someone who read the DDD condition page sees a different message than someone who hasn't visited the site yet.
Personalized performance
Creative, copy, and landing pages are dynamically aligned to each persona. Maria sees conservative-first messaging. James sees second-opinion framing. Every dollar works harder because every impression is relevant.
Section 06

Social Media — Trust at Scale

35% of patients choose a provider based on social presence. Consistent posting builds the trust layer underneath every other channel.

FS
Frisco Spine
@friscospine
"Degenerative disc disease" sounds alarming. But here's what it actually means — and why most people who have it don't need surgery. Your discs are shock absorbers that lose hydration over time. It's common, manageable, and for most patients treatable without the OR.

#DDD #SpineHealth #FriscoSpine
Awareness → Research. Links to DDD condition page. Persona: Maria A. + Yolanda D.
Age 38–62 · DFW organic reach · Interests: back pain, PT, spine health
FS
Frisco Spine
@friscospine
MYTH: Spine surgery means months of recovery and a 50/50 shot.
FACT: Minimally invasive cervical fusion takes about 45 minutes. Most patients are home same day with a Band-Aid, not a brace.

#MythBusting #SpineSurgery
Consideration → Decision. Resolves fear delaying booking. Persona: James M.
Age 45–60 · Retargeting warm audience + health interests
FS
Frisco Spine
@friscospine
Board certified. Fellowship trained at Cedars-Sinai and MD Anderson. 17 years in Frisco. 400+ patient reviews.

When a referred patient searches for Dr. Salinas and finds this alongside strong reviews, the referral converts.
Referral validation. 61% would decline a referral on weak social presence. This closes the loop. Persona: Barbara T.

30 posts/mo, auto-scheduled
10 posts per platform (Facebook, Instagram, Google). Quarterly calendar planned in advance. Zero operational overhead for the practice.
Section 07

Website Redesign — Three Directions

Three design concepts, each emphasizing a different strategic angle. All share the same brand system, consultation-first CTA, and trust signal architecture.

Option A
Capabilities-Led
Leads with DDD specialization and the Diagnose → Treat → Cure care architecture. Emphasizes the multidisciplinary team, condition library, and credentials.
View Full Design →
Option B
SEO & Authority-Led
Leads with search-optimized content and second-opinion pathway. Heavy on FAQs, patient stories, and clinical results. Built for AI Overviews and snippets.
View Full Design →
Option C
Location & Trust-Led
Leads with the one-stop Frisco facility and local presence. World-class care in your backyard, 3-step recovery path, specialized spine center.
View Full Design →
Section 08

Condition-Specific Pages

Each condition gets a dedicated, SEO-optimized page bridging "my back hurts" to "I need to see this doctor."

Primary — DDD

Degenerative Disc Disease

The flagship condition page targeting 15+ keywords patients actively search.

Patient-facing language — "What is DDD?" not "lumbar spondylosis"
Conservative-first structure — symptoms → non-surgical → when surgery is right
FAQ schema markup — captures Google AI Overviews
Embedded consultation CTA — form mid-page and bottom
View DDD Page Prototype →
Recommended pages (6–8 initial)
Degenerative Disc Disease — primary
Herniated Disc / Sciatica
Spinal Stenosis
Failed Back Surgery / Revision
Cervical Myelopathy
Scoliosis
Spondylolisthesis — Phase 2
Spinal Tumors — Phase 2
Why this matters: Hospitals get 68% of inbound organic traffic from informational content. Each page is a search magnet, trust builder, and conversion path.
